label: argentina-france-di-maria
title: Di María 36' (Argentina v France, final)
summary: Argentina build the third goal from Tagliafico's interception. Watch attention follow Mac Allister and Di María through the chain — and how P(score) climbs as the defence is spread before any pass on the goal is made.

label: argentina-france-mbappe-volley
title: Mbappé 81' volley (France v Argentina, final)
summary: Mbappé's second goal in 97 seconds, this one off the volley. Watch P(concede) for Argentina spike as France break in transition before the strike.

label: netherlands-usa-memphis
title: Memphis 10' (Netherlands v USA, R16)
summary: Netherlands' opener. Memphis at the end of a 20-pass Dutch sequence.

label: japan-spain-doan
title: Doan 48' (Japan v Spain, group stage)
summary: Japan equalize from a press-and-recover sequence — Doan's strike that knocked Spain off top of the group.

label: argentina-australia-messi
title: Messi 35' (Argentina v Australia, R16)
summary: Argentina build out of their own half; Otamendi flicks it to Messi at the top of the box; Messi finishes low past Ryan.

label: croatia-japan-perisic
title: Perišić 55' (Croatia v Japan, R16)
summary: Croatia equalize through Perišić's header. The Modrić–Brozović–Kovacić engine works the ball wide and the cross arrives where Japan's back line is least set — chemistry through the midfield triangle.

label: morocco-portugal-en-nesyri
title: En-Nesyri 42' (Morocco v Portugal, QF)
summary: Morocco's opener and the goal that put them into the semis. En-Nesyri rises above Diogo Costa on a cross from the right — watch the off-ball runner shape and the wide build-up.

label: near-miss-netherlands-janssen
title: Janssen 4' blocked (Senegal v Netherlands, group stage)
summary: A near-miss: Netherlands work the ball into Senegal's box and P(score) climbs above 0.9 before Janssen's shot is blocked. The probability collapses as Senegal clears — the model picks up on chemistry that doesn't end in a goal.

label: bad-chemistry-australia-argentina
title: Australia turnover 78' (Argentina v Australia, R16)
summary: What a breakdown looks like in the model. Australia start in possession with a positive net, lose the ball to Argentina, briefly win it back, then lose it again — net (P_score − P_concede) flips repeatedly between +0.9 and −0.9 as possession ping-pongs.
